title: Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan
tagline: Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: The Shankaragaurishvara Temple is a 9th century Hindu temple attributed to the Utpala dynasty.
overview.description: The Shankaragaurishvara Temple is a 9th century Hindu temple attributed to the Utpala dynasty. The temple is dedicated to the Hindu god, Shiva and located in Pattan, in the Baramulla district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The temple was built by king Shankaravarman of the Utpala dynasty of Kashmir, who ruled between 883 and 902 AD. The temple is in a dilapidated condition and worship is no longer conducted. It is built in a style similar to the Shankaracharya Temple.

topPlaces.name: Pattan
topPlaces.description: Pattan, is a sub-district town and a municipal community in Baramulla district within the Indian Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Palhallan
topPlaces.description: Palhallan is a census village in Baramula district, Jammu & Kashmir, India. As per the 2011 Census of India, Palhallan has a total population of 14,206 people including 7,399 males and 6,807 females with a literacy rate of 45.28%.

topPlaces.name: Hanjiwera
topPlaces.description: Hanjiwera or HeanzYor is a notified area and village in Singhpora, Pattan, Baramulla district in Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is located 31 km (19 mi) to the east from the district headquarters in Baramulla and 23 km (14 mi) from union territory capital, Srinagar.

topPlaces.name: Wanigam Bala
topPlaces.description: Wanigam Bala is a village in Baramulla district in the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is 8 km (5.0 mi) away from Pattan, the tehsil capital.

topPlaces.name: Singhpora
topPlaces.description: Singhpora or Singhpur is a town and municipality located on the Srinagar–Baramulla road (part of National Highway 44) in Baramulla district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Babawayil
topPlaces.description: Babawayil, also known as Baba Wayil and The dowry free village is a small village in Indian-administered Kashmir's Ganderbal district, around 30 km away from Srinagar, and at an elevation of 1786 meters (5859 feet). It is known for its banning of dowries.

topPlaces.name: 1885 Kashmir earthquake
topPlaces.description: The 1885 Kashmir earthquake, also known as the Baramulla earthquake occurred on 30 May in Srinagar. It had an estimated moment magnitude of Mw 6.3–6.8 and maximum Medvedev–Sponheuer–Karnik scale intensity of VIII (Damaging).

topPlaces.name: Purni Suder Shah
topPlaces.description: Purni Suder Shah or Purn Suder Shah, also known as The Dwelling Place Of Sufi Saint Suder Shah, is a village in the Budgam district in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It lies in the Narbal tehsil of district Budgam, on the eastern side of Mazhom railway station.

items.note: Pattan, is a sub-district town and a municipal community in Baramulla district within the Indian Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

Palhallan is a census village in Baramula district, Jammu & Kashmir, India. As per the 2011 Census of India, Palhallan has a total population of 14,206 people including 7,399 males and 6,807 females with a literacy rate of 45.28%.

Hanjiwera or HeanzYor is a notified area and village in Singhpora, Pattan, Baramulla district in Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is located 31 km (19 mi) to the east from the district headquarters in Baramulla and 23 km (14 mi) from union territory capital, Srinagar.
Wanigam Bala is a village in Baramulla district in the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is 8 km (5.0 mi) away from Pattan, the tehsil capital.
Singhpora or Singhpur is a town and municipality located on the Srinagar–Baramulla road (part of National Highway 44) in Baramulla district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

Babawayil, also known as Baba Wayil and The dowry free village is a small village in Indian-administered Kashmir's Ganderbal district, around 30 km away from Srinagar, and at an elevation of 1786 meters (5859 feet). It is known for its banning of dowries.

The 1885 Kashmir earthquake, also known as the Baramulla earthquake occurred on 30 May in Srinagar. It had an estimated moment magnitude of Mw 6.3–6.8 and maximum Medvedev–Sponheuer–Karnik scale intensity of VIII (Damaging).

Purni Suder Shah or Purn Suder Shah, also known as The Dwelling Place Of Sufi Saint Suder Shah, is a village in the Budgam district in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It lies in the Narbal tehsil of district Budgam, on the eastern side of Mazhom railway station.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (28 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (135 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan?
faq.a: Stays in Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shankaragaurishvara Temple, Pattan?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pattan, Palhallan, Hanjiwera, Wanigam Bala, Singhpora.
